verdict_reason: All nine Sanskrit properties are preserved as distinct in the engineering analog. The doctrinally critical sthāṇu / acala distinction (which English translations frequently collapse to a single 'unchanging/immovable' pair) is preserved by the analog: sthāṇu becomes 'stable — does not drift' (structural stability) while acala becomes 'immovable — versioning by addition not mutation' (no-displacement). Shankara's deductive-chain reading appears in the cumulative structure (the properties are presented in order, building on each other). Ramanuja's pervasion-by-subtlety reading appears in 'all-pervading — every service must honour it' (the contract pervades implementations). Both readings are flagged in the disagreements_among_translators field and are not conflated in the engineering analog. PASS.
